101.reason: [This DFS implementation does not correctly traverse neighbors in ascending order. It attempts to find the smallest unvisited neighbor in each iteration of the outer loop, but the logic is flawed: it restarts the search from 'q' (current position) instead of from the head of the adjacency list, and may skip or repeat vertices. Also, it doesn't ensure that all neighbors are visited exactly once in sorted order.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This DFS implementation does not traverse neighbors in ascending order correctly. It tries to find the smallest unvisited neighbor in the entire remaining list each time, but after visiting one, it doesn't continue with the next smallest from the original list. Also, it may revisit nodes or skip some due to flawed logic. The standard DFS should iterate through neighbors in sorted order and recursively visit unvisited ones.]

162.reason: [This BFS implementation is fundamentally incorrect. Instead of enqueueing all unvisited neighbors at once (as standard BFS does), it finds only the smallest unvisited neighbor, enqueues it, and repeats until no more neighbors—effectively turning BFS into a strange hybrid that doesn't explore level-by-level. This violates BFS semantics and fails to visit all neighbors properly in sorted order.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This BFS implementation is fundamentally flawed. Instead of enqueuing all unvisited neighbors in sorted order, it only enqueues the smallest unvisited neighbor repeatedly until none left. This misses other neighbors and does not perform standard BFS. The inner while loop tries to find one min per iteration, but doesn't enqueue all unvisited neighbors. Correct BFS should iterate through all neighbors (in sorted order) and enqueue each unvisited one once.]

52.reason: [The input order is wrong: it should read 'n m' (vertices edges), but reads 'm n'. This causes m to be the number of vertices and n the number of edges, but later code uses m as vertex count correctly. However, in the test case input "3 3", m becomes 3 (correct vertex count), so this may not be the direct cause of WA, but still a potential confusion. However, the real issue lies elsewhere.]
